title: ICE Reissues $10 Million Reward for Los Chapitos Leader Iván Archivaldo Guzmán Salazar
detail: U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) and the U.S. Embassy in Mexico on April 14, 2026 formally reiterated and amplified the $10 million reward for information leading to the arrest or conviction of Iván Archivaldo Guzmán Salazar, known as 'El Chapito,' the eldest surviving son of Joaquín 'El Chapo' Guzmán and co-leader of the Chapitos faction of the Sinaloa Cartel. His last known location is Culiacán, Sinaloa. He faces federal charges in the United States including conspiracy to distribute controlled substances (fentanyl and methamphetamine), continuing criminal enterprise, and money laundering. The reward announcement came amid intensified U.S. enforcement against the Sinaloa Cartel under the Trump administration's narco-terrorism strategy, and in the same week as the Lancaster/Salazar family arrests for Sinaloa-linked trafficking.

media.caption: ICE offers $10 million reward for Chapitos leader Iván Archivaldo Guzmán Salazar

title: Four Members of California Family Arrested for Sinaloa Cartel Fentanyl and Ghost Gun Trafficking
detail: Federal authorities in Los Angeles arrested four members of the Salazar family on a 29-count federal indictment for operating a Sinaloa Cartel-linked drug and firearms trafficking ring in Lancaster and Hesperia, California. The arrested individuals were: José Luis 'Oso' Salazar-Cruz (44, Lancaster), Alfonso 'Pirate' Salazar (46, Lancaster), José Manuel 'Lil Oso' Salazar (22, Lancaster), and Jorge Humberto 'Sharky' Salazar (43, Hesperia). A fifth suspect, José Ángel López Paniagua (23, Littlerock), remained at large. The ring sourced fentanyl and methamphetamine from Sinaloa Cartel members in Tijuana and Mexicali, smuggled across the border in trailers, and also trafficked ghost guns and unregistered rifles. The conspiracy ran from February 2024 through December 2025. Three of the four arrested are described as undocumented Mexican nationals. Each faces a maximum penalty of life in federal prison. The arrests demonstrated the continued operational reach of Sinaloa Cartel distribution networks in California despite the ongoing factional civil war in Culiacán.

media.caption: Sinaloa Cartel-linked family arrested in Lancaster, California for drug and gun trafficking
